title: Engineering handbook baseline
summary: Agents writing, reviewing, or testing durable repository code MUST apply the engineering baseline unrolled into the active invocation for scope, maintainability, testing, validation reporting, and operator authority.
instructions: Agents writing, reviewing, or testing code MUST apply the complete unrolled engineering guidance included with this policy before completion.
Implementation, review, and QA work MUST apply the unrolled rules for bounded scope, maintainability, testing, validation reporting, and operator authority.
Agents MUST interpret core functionality as every new or changed deterministic observable behavior and acceptance criterion, including the primary success path and each material branch.

Agents MUST interpret likely edge cases as reasonably reachable boundary, empty, missing, invalid, and error states implied by the changed input shapes, contracts, nullable values, or prior regressions; exhaustive permutations of equivalent inputs are not required.

Implementation work MUST add unit tests for core functionality and likely edge cases unless the behavior cannot be isolated meaningfully. In that case, the nearest appropriate integration or regression test MUST be added and the substitution MUST be explained.

Workflow-local automation under runtime/logs/workflows/<run-id>/scripts/ MAY be throwaway and need not satisfy the broader readability or abstraction guidance unless it is promoted into durable repository code.
Agents MUST treat the unrolled engineering guidance as supporting repository guidance and MUST NOT use it as a replacement for the active invocation contract.
